The ICU is larger than the lab.

The wing is more modern than before.
The lab is smaller than the ICU.
Good morning, Doctor Carter. We have 75 medical groups.
Good morning, Graziele. Which group is the large one?
Good. And the emergency team?
The emergency team is faster than the lab.
And the new wing? Is it good?
Yes. The new wing is more modern than the old wing.
Excellent. And what is the key point?
Patient safety is more important than speed.
Perfect. You present the groups very well.

Hi Graziele, this is Doctor Carter. Welcome to Block 3. Here is a quick tour of our groups. The ICU is larger than the lab. The emergency team is faster than the lab, too. Our new wing is more modern than the old wing. But remember, patient safety is more important than speed. Tomorrow you present three groups. Compare them with than. You will do great.

Hi Graziele, it is Ana. A quick tip before you present the medical groups. To compare two things, use than. For short words like large and fast, just say larger and faster. For long words like important and modern, use more. Say more important and more modern. One clear fact for each group. Keep it simple. You will do great.

Graziele presents a medical group. First, the name: this is the ICU. Then, the size: the ICU is larger than the lab. Next, the speed: the team is faster than before. After that, the equipment: the machines are more modern than the old ones. Finally, the point: but safety is more important than speed.
